---
title: "SimiC-Suite"
description: "A reproducible framework for phenotype-specific gene regulatory network analysis from single-cell RNA-seq data."
---

::: {.graphical-abstract}
![SimiC-Suite graphical abstract](figures/simic_suite_graphical_abstract.png){fig-alt="Graphical abstract summarizing the SimiC-Suite workflow from single-cell RNA-seq inputs through SimiCPipeline, SimiCviz, and downstream reporting."}
:::

## Overview

SimiC-Suite is a software suite for phenotype-specific gene regulatory network (GRN) analysis from single-cell RNA-seq data. It brings together complementary tools for GRN inference, Regulon Activity Score (RAS) calculation, and downstream assessment and visualization.

## Suite Components

::: {.package-tabs .panel-tabset}

## SimiCPipeline

::: {.package-panel .pipeline-panel}

**SimiCPipeline** is the Python component of SimiC-Suite for phenotype-specific GRN inference and Regulon Activity Score (RAS) calculation.

| Feature | Description |
|---|---|
| Ecosystem | Python |
| Primary role | Infer phenotype-specific gene regulatory networks from single-cell RNA-seq data. |
| Main outputs | Label-specific GRN weights and Regulon Activity Score (RAS) values. |
| Suite position | Upstream inference and RAS calculation before downstream assessment and visualization. |

SimiCPipeline builds on the SimiC framework for phenotype-aware regulatory network analysis. Please cite the original SimiC work when using SimiCPipeline where appropriate.

[View SimiCPipeline on GitHub](https://github.com/ML4BM-Lab/SimiCPipeline){.btn .btn-primary}

:::

## SimiCviz

::: {.package-panel .viz-panel}

**SimiCviz** is the R / Bioconductor component of SimiC-Suite for tool-agnostic assessment and visualization of GRN outputs.

| Feature | Description |
|---|---|
| Ecosystem | R / Bioconductor |
| Primary role | Assess, compare, and visualize GRN outputs across inference workflows. |
| Main outputs | Reproducible tables, figures, summaries, and visualizations for GRN interpretation. |
| Suite position | Downstream analysis and reporting after GRN inference and RAS calculation. |

SimiCviz supports interpretation of GRN outputs in R-centered analysis workflows and is designed to be independent of a single GRN inference tool.

[View SimiCviz on GitHub](https://github.com/ML4BM-Lab/SimiCviz){.btn .btn-danger}

:::

:::
